title: Add runtime payload validation in bridge.ts event handlers
analysis:
The 6 payload-bearing handlers in subscribeToClaudeEvents (lines 170-193 of bridge.ts) cast event.payload with compile-time-only `as` assertions. If the Rust sidecar emits null, undefined, or a structurally wrong payload, undefined values propagate silently into the handler callback. The fix adds: (1) a shared isRecord() helper to reject null/undefined/non-object/array payloads, (2) per-handler typeof checks on required string fields (id, content, tool_name, message), and (3) tool_input normalization in the tool_call handler to coerce null/array to {}. Existing happy-path tests in claudeBridge.test.ts already verify correct mapping; new tests cover the malformed-payload edge cases. The meshManager.ts validateMeshData pattern (console.warn + early return) is the project convention for runtime data guards.

Post-review update (round 2): Two new blocking issues — (1) main advanced again past the branch, requiring another rebase, and (2) the mkdir-based lock fallback in tree-sitter-generate.sh has a stat-failure bypass: when both `stat -c %Y` and `stat -f %m` fail (e.g. NFS, BusyBox), `|| echo 0` produces epoch-0, making `_lock_age` always >> 120, which unconditionally removes active locks. Fix: use empty sentinel for failed stat, skip stale-removal when mtime is unknown.

Post-review update (round 3): Two blocking issues — (1) main advanced again, requiring yet another rebase, and (2) scripts/test_tree_sitter_generate.sh calls `sha256sum` directly on lines 51 and 111, which fails on macOS where only `shasum -a 256` is available. The generate script already has a portable `compute_sha256()` helper but the test script bypasses it. With `set -euo pipefail` active, the test script aborts immediately on macOS. Fix: define a portable `compute_hash()` helper in the test script mirroring the generate script's `compute_sha256()` logic, and replace both bare `sha256sum` calls.

Post-review update (round 4): merge_ff_failed again — main advanced during the merge attempt. Branch is already rebased on latest origin/main (verified: origin/main is ancestor of HEAD). This is a timing race during the merge window, not a code issue. Adding rebase-retry step.

Post-review update (round 5): merge_ff_failed yet again — 5th occurrence of main advancing during the merge window. Branch verified rebased on origin/main after fresh fetch. All code changes are complete; this is purely a merge timing issue with high-velocity main branch. Adding step-15 for another rebase-and-retry.

Post-review update (round 6): merge_ff_failed for the 6th time — main continues to advance during the merge window. All code changes remain complete and correct. This is exclusively a merge timing race with a high-velocity main branch. Adding step-16 for another rebase-and-retry.

Post-review update (round 7): merge_ff_failed for the 7th time — main advanced during the merge window yet again. All code changes remain complete and passing. This is a persistent merge timing race with a high-velocity main branch. Adding step-17 for another rebase-and-retry.

Post-review update (round 8): merge_ff_failed for the 8th time — main advanced during the merge window yet again (c7a75c7a landed on main). Branch is verified already rebased on latest origin/main (origin/main is ancestor of HEAD). All code changes remain complete and passing. This is purely a persistent merge timing race. Adding step-18 for fetch + rebase-if-needed + retry.

Post-review update (round 9): Two blocking issues — (1) merge_ff_failed for the 9th time, and (2) infinite_loop in tree-sitter-generate.sh:99-102 — when `rmdir "$LOCK_DIR"` fails on line 101 (e.g. permission-restricted NFS, container bind-mounts with uid mismatch), `|| true` silently swallows the error and `continue` re-enters the while loop. Since `_lock_attempts` stays >= 75, every subsequent failed `mkdir` immediately re-enters the stale-check block, tries `rmdir` again, fails again, and `continue`s — spinning in a tight busy-loop with no sleep and no exit condition. The `sleep 1` on line 109 is only reached when `_lock_attempts < 75`, so once past the threshold the script burns 100% CPU. Fix: replace `rmdir ... || true` + bare `continue` with `rmdir ... || { sleep 1; continue; }` so the loop always sleeps between attempts even when rmdir fails, and add `_lock_attempts=0` after successful rmdir so the retry window reopens properly.

steps.description: Write failing tests: null/undefined/non-object payloads silently dropped for all 6 payload-bearing handlers. For each of claude-text-delta, claude-thinking-delta, claude-tool-call, claude-tool-result, claude-done, claude-error: simulate event with payload=null, payload=undefined, payload='string', and payload=42. Assert handler() is never called. Group under a new describe('payload validation guards') block in claudeBridge.test.ts.

Add a private isRecord() helper to bridge.ts: `function isRecord(v: unknown): v is Record<string, unknown> { return v != null && typeof v === 'object' && !Array.isArray(v); }`. Add `if (!isRecord(event.payload)) return;` as the first line in all 6 payload-bearing handler callbacks. This makes step-1 tests pass while preserving all existing happy-path behavior.

Write failing tests: missing or wrong-type required string fields → handler not called. Test cases: (a) text_delta with id=123 (number instead of string), (b) text_delta with missing content field, (c) thinking_delta with content=null, (d) tool_call with tool_name missing, (e) tool_result with tool_name=42, (f) done with id=undefined, (g) error with message=[] (array instead of string), (h) error with missing id. Each should call handler 0 times.

Refactor the 6 handler callbacks to validate required string fields before constructing the OutboundMessage. For each handler: destructure from event.payload, check `typeof field !== 'string'` for each required string field, return early if any check fails. Handlers: text_delta needs id+content, thinking_delta needs id+content, tool_call needs id+tool_name, tool_result needs id+tool_name, done needs id, error needs id+message. Add console.warn on validation failure for debuggability (following meshManager pattern).

Write failing tests for tool_input normalization in tool_call handler: (a) tool_input=null → handler called with tool_input={}, (b) tool_input=[1,2,3] (array) → handler called with tool_input={}, (c) tool_input=undefined → handler called with tool_input={}, (d) tool_input='string' → handler called with tool_input={}. Verify that valid tool_input={path:'/f'} still passes through unchanged (existing test covers this but add explicit assertion).

In the tool_call handler, after field validation, normalize tool_input: `const tool_input = isRecord(p.tool_input) ? (p.tool_input as Record<string, unknown>) : {};`. This coerces null, undefined, arrays, and primitives to an empty object. Run full test suite to verify all existing happy-path tests plus new validation tests pass.

[review-fix: merge_ff_failed] Rebase worktree branch onto latest origin/main. Fetch origin, rebase task/569 onto origin/main, resolve any conflicts (the task's changes are in gui/src/bridge.ts and gui/src/__tests__/claudeBridge.test.ts — conflicts are unlikely since main advanced in unrelated areas). Verify tests still pass after rebase.

[review-fix: portability_failure] In scripts/tree-sitter-generate.sh, replace the GNU-only `sha256sum` call on line 34 with a portable hash computation. Use `command -v sha256sum` to detect GNU coreutils, fall back to `shasum -a 256` for macOS, and exit with a clear error if neither is available. The hash function should be defined once (e.g. `compute_sha256()`) and called at line 34 for GRAMMAR_HASH. Both paths produce the same output format: hex hash followed by filename, so the `awk '{print $1}'` extraction remains unchanged.

[review-fix: race_condition] In scripts/tree-sitter-generate.sh, wrap the generation block (staleness re-check through stamp write, roughly lines 39-79) with flock-based advisory locking. Open a file descriptor on `$TS_DIR/src/.generate.lock`, acquire an exclusive lock via `flock -x`, then re-evaluate the staleness check inside the lock (to handle the lost-update case where another process already regenerated while we waited). Release happens automatically when the subshell/script exits. This prevents concurrent builds from corrupting parser.c/grammar.json/node-types.json via interleaved writes.

[review-fix: lock_bypass_on_stat_failure] In scripts/tree-sitter-generate.sh line 90, fix the stale-lock age computation so that stat failure does not unconditionally trigger lock removal. Currently `|| echo 0` produces epoch-0 when both `stat -c %Y` and `stat -f %m` fail, making `_lock_age` always >> 120 and removing active locks on unsupported platforms. Fix: (1) Compute `_lock_mtime` separately: `_lock_mtime=$(stat -c %Y "$LOCK_DIR" 2>/dev/null || stat -f %m "$LOCK_DIR" 2>/dev/null || echo '')`. (2) Guard the age check: `if [ -n "$_lock_mtime" ]; then _lock_age=$(( $(date +%s) - _lock_mtime )); if [ "$_lock_age" -gt 120 ]; then ... rmdir ... continue; fi; fi`. (3) If `_lock_mtime` is empty (stat failed), fall through to the existing hard-failure `exit 1` — the lock cannot be verified as stale, so we must not remove it.

[review-fix: merge_ff_failed] Rebase worktree branch onto latest origin/main (main advanced again since last rebase in step-7). Fetch origin, rebase task/569 onto origin/main, resolve any conflicts. The task's changes span gui/src/bridge.ts, gui/src/__tests__/claudeBridge.test.ts, and scripts/tree-sitter-generate.sh — conflicts are unlikely since main typically advances in unrelated areas. Verify tests still pass after rebase.

[review-fix: portability_failure in test script] In scripts/test_tree_sitter_generate.sh, replace the two direct `sha256sum` calls on lines 51 and 111 with a portable hash helper. Define a `compute_hash()` function at the top of the test script (after variable declarations, before Test 1) that mirrors the generate script's `compute_sha256()` logic: `compute_hash() { if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then sha256sum "$1"; elif command -v shasum >/dev/null 2>&1; then shasum -a 256 "$1"; else echo "ERROR: neither sha256sum nor shasum found" >&2; exit 1; fi; }`. Then replace line 51 `expected_hash=$(sha256sum "$TS_DIR/grammar.js" | awk '{print $1}')` with `expected_hash=$(compute_hash "$TS_DIR/grammar.js" | awk '{print $1}')`, and replace line 111 `real_hash=$(sha256sum "$TS_DIR/grammar.js" | awk '{print $1}')` with `real_hash=$(compute_hash "$TS_DIR/grammar.js" | awk '{print $1}')`. This ensures the test script works on macOS (where only `shasum -a 256` is available) and doesn't abort with 'command not found' under `set -euo pipefail`.

[review-fix: merge_ff_failed] Rebase worktree branch onto latest origin/main (main advanced again since last rebase in step-11). Fetch origin, rebase task/569 onto origin/main, resolve any conflicts. The task's changes span gui/src/bridge.ts, gui/src/__tests__/claudeBridge.test.ts, scripts/tree-sitter-generate.sh, and scripts/test_tree_sitter_generate.sh. Verify tests still pass after rebase.

[review-fix: merge_ff_failed] Rebase worktree branch onto latest origin/main (main advanced again during the merge window since step-13). Fetch origin, rebase task/569 onto origin/main, resolve any conflicts. The branch is already verified as rebased on origin/main (origin/main is ancestor of HEAD) so this may be a no-op rebase — but re-verify after fetch in case main advanced further. The task's changes span gui/src/bridge.ts, gui/src/__tests__/claudeBridge.test.ts, scripts/tree-sitter-generate.sh, and scripts/test_tree_sitter_generate.sh. Verify tests still pass after rebase.

[review-fix: merge_ff_failed] Rebase worktree branch onto latest origin/main (5th merge-window race — main advanced again during the merge attempt after step-14). Fetch origin with depth sufficient to find merge-base, rebase task/569 onto origin/main, resolve any conflicts if present. The task's changes span gui/src/bridge.ts, gui/src/__tests__/claudeBridge.test.ts, scripts/tree-sitter-generate.sh, and scripts/test_tree_sitter_generate.sh — conflicts remain unlikely as main advances in unrelated areas. Verify origin/main is ancestor of HEAD after rebase. Run gui tests (`npx vitest run` in gui/) to confirm no regressions.

[review-fix: merge_ff_failed] Rebase worktree branch onto latest origin/main (6th merge-window race — main advanced again during the merge attempt after step-15). Fetch origin, rebase task/569 onto origin/main, force-push updated branch. All code changes are complete; this is purely a merge timing issue with a high-velocity main branch. Verify origin/main is ancestor of HEAD after rebase. Run gui tests (`npx vitest run` in gui/) to confirm no regressions.

[review-fix: merge_ff_failed] Rebase worktree branch onto latest origin/main (7th merge-window race — main advanced again during the merge attempt after step-16). Fetch origin, rebase task/569 onto origin/main, force-push updated branch. All code changes are complete and verified; this is purely a persistent merge timing race with a high-velocity main branch. Verify origin/main is ancestor of HEAD after rebase. Run gui tests (`npx vitest run` in gui/) to confirm no regressions.

[review-fix: merge_ff_failed] 8th merge-window race — main advanced again (c7a75c7a landed) during the merge attempt after step-17. Fetch origin, check if origin/main is still ancestor of HEAD. If not, rebase task/569 onto origin/main and force-push. If already an ancestor (verified current state), the branch is ready — just needs the merge to be re-attempted without main advancing in between. All code changes are complete and verified. No conflicts expected — the new main commit (c7a75c7a refactor(gui): centralize validatePayload) touches different code paths than our bridge.ts validation guards.

[review-fix: infinite_loop] In scripts/tree-sitter-generate.sh lines 99-103, fix the tight busy-loop when `rmdir` fails in the stale-lock removal path. Currently line 101 is `rmdir "$LOCK_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true` followed by bare `continue` on line 102. When rmdir fails (e.g. on NFS or container bind-mounts with uid mismatch where the process can stat but not remove the directory), `|| true` swallows the error, `continue` re-enters the while loop, and since `_lock_attempts` stays >= 75, every iteration immediately re-enters the stale-check block — spinning with no sleep and no exit. Fix: (1) Replace `rmdir "$LOCK_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true` + bare `continue` with: `if rmdir "$LOCK_DIR" 2>/dev/null; then _lock_attempts=0; continue; fi`. On successful rmdir, reset `_lock_attempts=0` so the retry window reopens (next `mkdir` attempt gets the normal sleep-1 backoff if it still fails). (2) If rmdir fails, do NOT continue — instead fall through past the stale-lock block to the existing `sleep 1` on line 109 (or add `sleep 1` explicitly before continuing). This ensures the loop always sleeps between attempts even when rmdir cannot remove the lock. (3) Verify with `bash -n scripts/tree-sitter-generate.sh` that syntax is valid after the edit.

[review-fix: merge_ff_failed] 9th merge-window race — main advanced again during the merge attempt after step-18. Fetch origin, rebase task/569 onto origin/main if needed (check if origin/main is ancestor of HEAD first). Force-push updated branch. All code changes including the new infinite_loop fix from step-19 must be included. Verify origin/main is ancestor of HEAD after rebase.

design_decisions.decision: Use silent return (not throw) for malformed payloads
design_decisions.rationale: Event handlers should be resilient — throwing would crash the listener loop and prevent subsequent valid events from being processed. The task description says 'undefined values propagate silently' is the bug; the fix is to drop the malformed event, not crash.

Follows the existing meshManager.ts pattern (console.warn for invalid data). Silent drops without logging would make debugging harder. The warn is lightweight and only fires on actual malformed events.

design_decisions.decision: Normalize tool_input to {} instead of rejecting the entire tool_call event
design_decisions.rationale: A tool_call event with a valid id and tool_name but null tool_input still carries useful information (the tool was called). Normalizing to {} is safer than dropping the entire event. The UI can still show the tool call happened.

design_decisions.decision: Shared isRecord() helper instead of inline typeof checks
design_decisions.rationale: All 6 handlers need the same null/undefined/array/primitive rejection for the payload object. A shared helper avoids duplication and ensures consistent behavior. Kept private to bridge.ts (not exported) since it's an implementation detail.

design_decisions.decision: Validate all required string fields, not just id
design_decisions.rationale: The task says 'optionally validate required string fields'. Since we're adding guards anyway, validating all required strings (content, tool_name, message) provides comprehensive protection. The cost is minimal — one typeof check per field.

design_decisions.decision: Shell script fixes are impl-only steps (no TDD)
design_decisions.rationale: The tree-sitter-generate.sh fixes (portability, race condition) are bash script changes with no corresponding test framework in the project. Verification is done by running the script and checking bash syntax. TDD alternation doesn't apply to infrastructure shell scripts.

design_decisions.decision: Define compute_sha256() helper rather than inline if/elif
design_decisions.rationale: Encapsulating the portable hash detection in a function keeps the main script flow clean and makes the fallback logic testable in isolation. The function is called once now but could be reused if additional files need hashing.

design_decisions.decision: Re-check staleness inside flock to handle lost-update
design_decisions.rationale: Without the re-check, two processes could both pass the staleness check before either acquires the lock, then both generate — wasting time. The double-check-locking pattern (check → lock → re-check) is the standard solution for this race.

design_decisions.decision: Use empty sentinel (not zero) for stat failure in stale-lock detection
design_decisions.rationale: The `|| echo 0` fallback when stat fails produces epoch-0, making lock_age always huge, which unconditionally removes locks on platforms where stat is unavailable. Using an empty string sentinel and skipping stale-removal when mtime is unknown is fail-safe: we refuse to remove a lock we cannot verify as stale, and instead hard-fail, which is the correct behavior for an unknown platform.

design_decisions.decision: Define compute_hash() in test script rather than sourcing from generate script
design_decisions.rationale: While the generate script's compute_sha256() could be sourced, the test script should be self-contained to avoid coupling test execution to generate script internals. A shared lib.sh would be over-engineering for two scripts. The helper is trivial (5 lines) and the duplication is acceptable for test isolation.

design_decisions.decision: Reset _lock_attempts on successful rmdir rather than just adding sleep
design_decisions.rationale: The reviewer's minimal fix (sleep 1 before continue) prevents CPU burn but leaves _lock_attempts permanently >= 75, meaning every subsequent iteration re-enters the stale-check path. Resetting _lock_attempts=0 after successful stale-lock removal reopens the normal retry window with proper backoff, which is the correct semantic: the stale lock is gone, we should retry acquiring fresh. If rmdir fails, we fall through to sleep 1 to prevent CPU burn while still eventually hitting the hard exit at attempt 75+N.

reuse.how: Reuse the same mock setup (vi.mock for @tauri-apps/api/core and @tauri-apps/api/event), the capturedHandler pattern for simulating events, and the ALL_EVENT_NAMES constant. New tests follow the exact same pattern as existing handler-mapping tests.
Follow the same pattern: validate, console.warn with descriptive message, return early. This is the established project convention for runtime data guards.
The type interfaces (TextDelta, ToolCall, etc.) define the required fields — validation checks mirror these interface definitions exactly. No new types needed.
The existing STALE flag + stamp file pattern is preserved. The flock fix wraps it rather than replacing it, and the portability fix only changes the hash computation, not the check logic.

The test script's compute_hash() helper mirrors the exact same fallback logic: try sha256sum first, fall back to shasum -a 256, error if neither found. Same output format (hash + filename) so the downstream awk extraction is unchanged.
